published_prior_art.cite: AngristImbens1995: ordered/variable-intensity IV estimands are weighted average causal responses under monotonicity

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2: finite latent-response LP sharp interval, but for dynamic assignment-memory IV rather than ordinal defier caps

published_prior_art.cite: BalkePearl1997: sharp latent-response-type bounds via linear programming for binary-IV imperfect compliance

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_late_bounded_defiers/one_sided_compliance is binary and cannot express shared 010/101 ordinal paths

published_prior_art.cite: HuberMellace2012: three-valued-instrument section names switch-from-compliance-to-defiance types, but does not give bounded-defier pairwise-vs-global sharp interval
MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2021: partial monotonicity for positive-weight multiple-IV 2SLS, not ordinal bounded-defier endpoint-facet kinks

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist missed Huber and Mellace, 'Relaxing monotonicity in the identification of LATEs' (2012, https://ux-tauri.unisg.ch/RePEc/usg/econwp/EWP-1212.pdf), whose non-binary-instrument section explicitly discusses three-level response types that switch from compliance to defiance.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem assumes eta_j=0 but cites only Assumptions 1--3; it must also invoke the defier-cap restriction q^-_j <= eta_j to imply q^-_j=0.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: P is locked as the full law of (Y,D,Z) with Y in [0,1], but the finite program matches only observed cell probabilities and means; either reduce P to first moments or use full latent outcome distributions/finite Y support.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: B_pair(P,eta) is not precise enough for a ratio target: the definition must say how adjacent numerator and denominator choices are jointly or independently optimized before forming the aggregate interval.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The 'first breakpoint as (eta_0,eta_1) increases from (0,0)' is path-dependent in two dimensions unless a ray, partial-order notion, or minimal-norm threshold is specified.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Huber--Mellace 2012 as the closest ordinal/non-binary defier comparison and state exactly why their local-monotonicity identification does not subsume bounded-defier sharp non-pasteability.
Repair the observable object in Theorem 2: either define P as the finite collection of cell probabilities and means, or restrict Y to finite/binary support and match the full cell distributions.
Replace the informal pairwise-pasting comparator with an explicit optimization problem for lower and upper endpoints, including denominator handling.
For Conjecture 2, define a one-dimensional sensitivity path eta=t v or a dominance-order breakpoint and add a rational J=2 witness target for Stage 0.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal before Stage 0: fix the theorem assumptions, formalize B_pair, clarify the eta-path kink, and add the missed Huber--Mellace ordinal-defier comparison.
